articles.title: Dreams Help Us Sleep Better
articles.summary: Scientists studied how dreams affect sleep. They found that vivid dreams can make sleep feel deeper and more restful. In the past, people thought deep sleep meant the brain was quiet, but dreaming happens when the brain is active. The researchers woke up adults during sleep and asked them about their dreams and how deep their sleep felt. They discovered that when people had clear and exciting dreams, they felt like they slept very deeply. This means dreams might help us feel more refreshed after sleeping. The study suggests that dreams could be like guardians that protect our sleep and make it feel better.

articles.title: Snow Flies: The Bug That Makes Its Own Heat in the Cold
articles.summary: Scientists found a special bug called a snow fly that lives in very cold places. Snow flies are insects that walk on snow to find friends and lay eggs. They can stay alive when it is freezing cold, even at -6 degrees Celsius. This is surprising because most insects cannot survive in such cold weather. Snow flies have two cool tricks. First, they make antifreeze proteins like Arctic fish to stop ice from hurting their cells. Second, they can create their own body heat, just like mammals do. This helps them stay active in the snow instead of hiding. The study helps us learn how animals adapt to extreme cold and might help protect things from freezing damage. It was done by scientists at Northwestern University and published in a science journal.

articles.title: Ancient Animal Discovery in New Zealand Cave
articles.summary: Scientists found old animal bones in a cave in New Zealand. These bones are about 1 million years old and include a new type of parrot related to the kākāpō, which is a big bird that cannot fly. The discovery has fossils from 12 bird species and 4 frog species, showing what New Zealand's nature was like long ago. The animals died out because of big changes in climate and volcanoes, even before people came to New Zealand. This helps scientists learn about ancient forests and animals that lived there. The team includes experts from Australia and New Zealand who studied the bones to understand this history.

articles.title: A New Machine That Can Study Billions of Molecules at Once
articles.summary: Scientists use a tool called mass spectrometry to find out what molecules are in a sample, like in cells or chemicals. Most machines today can only look at one or a few molecules at a time, which is slow and might miss rare ones. Researchers have made a new prototype called MultiQ-IT that can handle billions of molecules together, inspired by how cells move things through many small openings. This could help in studying single cells better, watching many reactions at once, and making drug discovery faster. The idea came from improving technology, similar to how computers and DNA sequencing got better by doing many things in parallel. This new device uses a cube with hundreds of openings to sort and analyze ions, which are charged molecules, all at the same time, making the process much more efficient.

articles.title: NASA's Plan to Use a Space Station Part for a Nuclear Mission
articles.summary: NASA has a new plan to use a part from a space station called Gateway for a nuclear-powered mission to Mars. Gateway was being built to orbit the Moon, but NASA is changing its focus to build a base on the Moon instead. The part, called the Power and Propulsion Element, is almost ready and will be used for a mission called SR-1 Freedom. This mission will test a nuclear reactor in space to help spacecraft travel farther, like to Mars. Nuclear power is useful in space because it works even where sunlight is weak. NASA hopes to launch SR-1 Freedom by 2028 to show that nuclear propulsion can work. This is important because the U.S. has only flown one nuclear reactor in space over 60 years ago, and new technology could make space travel faster and more efficient.

articles.title: Droughts Make Germs Stronger Against Medicine
articles.summary: A new study shows that when there is a drought, germs in the soil become better at fighting off antibiotics, which are medicines that kill bacteria. This happens because as the soil dries, the natural antibiotics made by soil bacteria get more concentrated, helping resistant germs survive. The researchers found that in dry areas, hospitals see more infections that are hard to treat with antibiotics. Since climate change is causing more droughts, this could make antibiotic resistance worse around the world. The study suggests we need to protect both our health and the planet to stop this problem.
